TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE SEWANEE AIRPORT SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES. MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES WAS OBSERVED ALONG LEAKY POND LANE. ALONG OTTER FALLS LANE IN THE MIDWAY COMMUNITY...A HOME SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WHEN THE CARPORT WAS DESTROYED AND THE CHIMNEY WAS DESTROYED BY FLYING DEBRIS. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO MOVE NORTHEAST PRODUCING MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES ALONG WITH SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE BEFORE MOVING INTO MARION COUNTY.

*** 3 INJ *** AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF TRIANA BLVD AND 9TH AVE...THEN TRACKED 4.4 MILES NORTHEAST BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GABOURY LN AND ROSALIE RIDGE RD. BOTH SOFT AND HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED...AS WERE WOODEN UTILITY POLES. MULTIPLE HOMES SUSTAINED SUBSTANTIAL ROOF DAMAGE BOTH FROM FALLING TREES AND TORNADIC WINDS.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF TORNADO SPOTTED IN THE FIVE POINTS AND CHAPMAN MOUNTAIN AREAS...WITH REPORTS OF DAMAGE...POWER LINE FLASHES.

AN EF-2 TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN ON HIGHWAY 31 JUST SOUTH OF THE COUNTY ROAD 601 INTERSECTION. THE TORNADO WAS 300 YARDS WIDE AND 6 MILES LONG. OVER 100 TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED. TWO CHICKEN HOUSES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND SEVERAL BARNS SUSTAINED MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE.

AN EF-3 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF DOWNTOWN ALBERTVILLE. MULTIPLE TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED IN THE CITY. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES. BP GAS STATION HAS BEEN DAMAGED ON HWY 431 ALONG WITH A GROCERY STORE. HOUSE DESTROYED ON HWY 75 ACROSS FROM AN ELEMENTARY SCHOOL. BARNS HAVE BEEN DESTROYED AS WELL. SEVERAL OTHER BUSINESSES HAVE BEEN DAMAGED.
POSSIBLE TORNADO NEAR DOWNTOWN ALBERTVILLE. MULTIPLE TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN. REPORTS OF ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES. BP GAS STATION HAS BEEN DAMAGED ON HWY 431.

AN EF-3 TORNADO MOVED ACROSS GERALDINE. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS REPORTED AROUND GERALDINE HIGH SCHOOL. POWER LINES AND POLES HAVE BEEN DOWNED ACROSS THE CITY. RIDGE CREST APPARTMENT COMPLEX SUSTAINED DAMAGE IN ADDITION TO OTHER BUILDINGS AND HOMES DAMAGED.

ON COUNTY ROAD 678...AN AREA APPROXIMATELY 20 FEET DEEP AND 40 FEET WIDE WAS WASHED AWAY. NATURAL GAS LINES...WATER MAINS...AND PHONE LINES WERE DESTROYED IN THE AREA.

AN EF3 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3 TO 4 MILES SOUTHEAST OF MENTONE...BETWEEN COUNTY ROAD 106 AND 117. PATH LENGTH WAS 2.0 MILES AND THE TORNADO WAS 1/4 MILE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND 140 MPH. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED AT MORNING GLORY COURT...WHERE SEVERAL SINGLE-WIDE MANUFACTURED HOMES WERE COMPLETELY DESTROYED. SEVERAL INJURIES OCCURRED WITH THIS TORNADO BUT AN EXACT NUMBER IS UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.

AN EF-3 TORNADO IMPACTED THE AREAS FROM MOUNT VERNON TO DOG TOWN IN EXTREME SE DEKALB COUNTY. PATH LENGTH WAS 7.3 MILES AND PATH WIDTH WAS 1/2 MILE AT ITS WIDEST POINT. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND 150 MPH. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 80 AND 55 WHERE A CHURCH AND A SMALLER HOUSE NEXT TO IT WERE BOTH DESTROYED. OAK TREES NEARBY WERE DEBARKED. ELSEWHERE...STRUCTURAL AND TREE DAMAGE WERE REPORTED. INJURIES OCCURRED BUT EXACT NUMBER UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.

SEVERAL TREES DOWN ALONG SWEARENGIN ROAD.
SEVERAL TREES SNAPPED ALONG SWEARENGIN ROAD. PONTOON BOAT THROWN INTO MOBILE HOME.
POWER LINES DOWN NEAR CENTER STAR AND KILLEN.
TREES DOWN ACROSS POWER LINES ON HIGHWAY 101 AT THE SOUTH END OF WHEELER DAM.
DECATUR POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED MULTIPLE POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN WITH SOME TRAFFIC SIGNALS OUT. STREET SIGN ALSO DOWN ON US 31 NORTH NEAR DECATUR.
NUMEROUS WATER COVERED ROADWAYS IN THE CITY OF ALBERTVILLE.
SEVERAL ROADS IN SCOTTSBORO HAVE BEEN CLOSED OR BARRICADED AND WATER HAS NEARED SEVERAL RESIDENCES.
THREE FEET OF WATER WAS COVERING THE HARDEES PARKING LOT ON BROAD STREET. TWO CARS DROVE INTO A FLOODED DITCH. A WALKWAY OVER THE DITCH WAS WASHED OUT.
FLOOD WATER WAS IMPACTING RESIDENCES ON HIGHWAY 79 IN HYTOP. PHOTOS OF FLOODING WERE RELAYED BY BROADCAST MEDIA. TIME ESTIMATED.
WATER RESCUES ARE UNDERWAY AT RESIDENCES NEAR MAPLE AVENUE IN SCOTTSBORO. WATER IS UP TO 4.5 FEET DEEP.

DRY CREEK WAS FLOODING A RUNWAY AT THE SCOTTSBORO MUNICIPAL AIRPORT WITH 3-4 INCHES OF WATER. THE AUTOMATED COOP WEATHER STATION AT THIS LOCATION WAS PARTIALLY SUBMERGED IN FLOOD WATERS. 6.68 INCHES OF RAIN WAS REPORTED BY A SECONDARY WEATHER STATION AT THE AIRPORT. THIS IS SUSPECTED TO BE UNDERESTIMATING THE RAINFALL TOTAL.
TREE DOWN ON POWER LINES IN THE RED OAK COMMUNITY WEST-SOUTHWEST OF PETERSBURG.
TREE DOWN ON A POWER LINE ON CORNFIELD LANE WEST OF MERIDIANVILLE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
TREES DOWN IN THE WALNUT GROVE AREA EAST OF HAZEL GREEN. A CAR RAN INTO ONE OF THE DOWNED TREES.
ROADS REPORTED FLOODED IN THE FALKVILLE AND EVA AREAS.
SMALL TREES LIMBS WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG CR 730 AND CR747 IN WELTI. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN THE OWEN COMMUNITY
1 INCH HAIL FOR THREE MINUTES IN ARAB
PORCH AND ROOF DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE IN ROGERSVILLE. TREE AND FENCE DAMAGE IN ADDITION TO STRUCTUAL DAMAGE TO A BARN ALSO SUSTAINED.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN LEXINGTON
ROOF TAKEN OFF A MOBILE HOME ON CANNON ROAD WEST OF ATHENS.
SEVERAL TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN AT A RESIDENCE ON GUNTER HOLLOW ROAD. MINOR DAMAGE DONE TO A BARN AND GREENHOUSE. AN ENCLOSED TRAILER WAS MOVED SEVERAL FEET.

TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN AND COVERING ROADWAYS NEAR THE INTERSECTIONS OF PLEASANT HILL AND CHESTNUT RIDGE RDS AND FLIPPO AND PLEASANT RIDGE RDS. REPORTS ALSO RECEIVED OF DAMAGE SUSTAINED TO A FEW RESIDENCES AND BARNS.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WAS DETERMINED IN A STORM SURVEY. THE PATH OF THE TORNADO WAS ABOUT 1.2 MILES WITH A 200 YARD PATH. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GRIFFIN AND HARRY HILL ROADS...AND CONTINUED THROUGH THE PLEASANT HILL COMMUNITY...BEFORE LIFTING NEAR MARKAY LANE. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS TO RESIDENTIAL HOMES...WITH TWO HOMES LOSING COVERED PORCHES.
SEVERAL TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN OR PARTIALLY UPROOTED ALONG VAUGHN BRIDGE ROAD JUST NORTHWEST OF HARTSELLE. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

MAJOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO BUILDINGS IN DOWNTOWN CULLMAN. WINDOWS AND BLOCKWALLS BLOWNOUT AND ROOF DAMAGE SUSTAINED TO NUMEROUS BUILDINGS. BIG TREES ALSO BLOWN DOWN NEAR THE CATHOLIC CHURCH. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A SHORT-LIVED TORNADO WITH AN ESTIMATED WIND UP TO 85 MPH...PATH LENGTH OF 1 MILE AND PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 231 AND HAYNES ROAD. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG WITH MINOR ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE TO A HOUSE. ADDITIONAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ON A GOLF COURSE. AS THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST...SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS WAS PRODUCED TO A FARM OUTBUILDING ON HUCKLEBERRY LANE.
POWER LINES DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF 12TH AVE AND HELEN DR. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ON ROADS NORTH OF ARAB INCLUDING MOBB HILL ROAD...SCANT CITY ROAD AND BRINDLEE MOUNTAIN PKWY. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.
TREES DOWN ACROSS CR 1856 IN CULLMAN COUNTY
TREE DOWN ON POWERLINE AND SCHOOL WITHOUT POWER IN CLAYSVILLE.
TREES DOWN ON A RV CAMPER AND LIGHT DAMAGE AT A CHALET. NO INJURIES REPORTED. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN THE COMMUNITY OF ASBURY.

A TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN BETWEEN SECTION AND DUTTON...JUST SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 71. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 18 AND 395. A TRAILER WAS FLIPPED ON ITS SIDE AND A SMALL SHED WAS DESTROYED. PORTIONS OF A ROOF/SIDING FROM A HOUSE WERE PULLED OFF AND SEVERAL SMALL TREES WERE SNAPPED.

AS PART OF THE SAME LINE...THIS TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR ROSALIE...BETWEEN COUNTY ROAD 456 AND 83. AT THIS INTERSECTION...THE PORCH/OVERHANG OF A HOUSE COLLAPSED AND SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 78. THE TORNADO THEN HIT 2 BARNS ALONG COUNTY ROAD 141... COLLAPSING THE ROOF AND WALLS OF ONE BARN. ADDITIONAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG WITH MINOR ROOF/STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A TRAILER AND SEVERAL FARM OUTBUILDINGS ALONG CR 803.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG CR322 WEST OF HENEGAR. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN FROM IDER TO HENEGAR. 2 POSSIBLE INJURIES DUE TO TREES FALLING ON CARS...ONE IN IDER AND ONE 2 MILES NORTH OF IDER. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.

AN EF-2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE TOWN OF SECTION IN EXTREME SOUTHERN JACKSON COUNTY AROUND 345 AM CDT AND TRACKED ENE ACROSS NORTHERN DEKALB COUNTY THROUGH 415 AM. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS SEEN BETWEEN PISGAH AND IDER WHERE SEVERAL RESIDENTIAL STRUCTURES AND FARM OUTBUILDINGS HAD MAJOR ROOF AND EXTERIOR WALL DAMAGE...WITH SOME WINDOWS AND DOORS BLOWN OUT. THE IDER HIGH SCHOOL FOOTBALL FIELD BLEACHERS AND SCOREBOARD WERE DESTROYED. THE BLEACHERS WERE CONCRETE WITH 1/2 REBAR.
DAMAGE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 431 AND HWY 75...AND ALONG HWY 75 TO THE INTERSECTION OF HUSTLEVILLE RD. THE KMART AT 431 AND HWY 75 SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.
TREES DOWN ON VEHICLES ON MEMORIAL DRIVE IN RAINSVILLE. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.
TREES DOWN ON A VEHICLE AT THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 117 AND HWY 75. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN BLOCKING CR 739, 774, AND GREEN ACRES RD.
TREES AND POWERLINE DOWN IN DUTTON. POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE.
A TREE WAS UPROOTED ONTO A HOUSE PRODUCING MINOR DAMAGE. TIME ESTIMATED.
SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG ROUGHLY A MILE OF CR 217 IN THE HILLSBORO COMMUNITY.

AN EF-1 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 20 NEAR CR 217...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 1.1 MILES AND PEAK WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED SEVERAL LARGE TREES BEFORE LIFTING WEST OF CR 400.
SEVERAL SMALL TREES WERE SNAPPED AND SMALL TO MEDIUM TREE LIMBS BROKEN BY STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GREEN COVE ROAD AND HIGHWAY 231.

AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN VERY BRIEFLY ALONG GREENBRIER ROAD NORTH OF THE INTERSTATE 565 INTERCHANGE...WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 500 YARDS AND PEAK WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO UPROOTED SEVERAL SMALL TREES AND BROKE SEVERAL LARGER TREE LIMBS.
BARN ROOF TORN OFF JUST NORTH OF MADISON BLVD ON COUNTY LINE ROAD.
PUBLIC REPORT OF TREE FALLING ON HOUSE ON JACKSON AVE IN MADISON.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG GUNTER AVENUE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 79/431 IN GUNTERSVILLE.
SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED IN A 4.25 MILE LONG PATH NEAR THE GERALDINE AND CHIGGER HILL COMMUNITIES. SEVERAL HOUSES SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE...WITH ONE PUSHED FROM ITS FOUNDATION.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG COUNTY ROAD 23 NEAR WHITON/ROGERS COMMUNITY. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

LIGHT...SPORADIC DAMAGE WAS PRODUCED ALONG HAMBY ROAD BY A WEAK TORNADO. DAMAGE WAS MAINLY CONFINED TO LOSS OF ROOF MATERIALS ON BARNS...AND LIGHT TREE DAMAGE. ONE LARGE TREE WAS SNAPPED BY THE TORNADO.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO HOUSES ALONG WITH NUMEROUS TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 227 BETWEEN GROVE OAK AND GERALDINE. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE ALONG COUNTY ROAD 333. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
A TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN ONTO HIGHWAY 117 NEAR MENTONE.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS DEMOLISHED TWO BARNS AT A FARM. ONE OF THE METAL BARNS WHICH WAS MOUNTED IN CONCRETE WAS TORN UP AND LIFTED ONTO AN ADJACENT FARM BUILDING.
TREES WERE KNOCKED DOWN AND BLOCKED THE INTERSECTION OF COLLEGE ROAD AND MAIN STREET IN DUTTON. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A TREE WAS KNOCKED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 71 AND CR 47 NEAR DUTTON. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A TREE WAS KNOCKED DOWN ALONG CR 141 NEAR IDER. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN IN LITTLEVILLE.
TREES DOWN ON GILLESPIE ROAD IN MADISON.
SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER COVERED PORTIONS OF SANDLIN ROAD AND HAY DRIVE.
SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER COVERED THE ROADS ON BOTH CEDAR LAKE ROAD AND CENTRAL PARKWAY.
PENNY LANE AND QUINCE DRIVE WERE REPORTED FLOODED.
PORTIONS OF NANCE FORD ROAD IN THE WESTERN PORTION OF HARTSELLE WERE BARRICADED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
PORTIONS OF HIGHWAY 36 ON THE WESTERN PORTION OF HARSELLE WERE BARRICADED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
PORTIONS OF FOREST CHAPEL ROAD WERE BARRICADED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
SEVERAL ROADS HAD SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER FLOWING OVER THEM IN THE MOORES MILL AREA. IN ADDITION, A MOTORISTS WAS STRANDED DUE TO HIGH WATER ON THE 300 BLOCK OF NAUGHER ROAD.

A BRIDGE APPROXIMATELY 1/2 MILE SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 36 ON GUM SPRINGS ROAD WAS FLOODED.
SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER WAS REPORTED FLOWING OVER THE ROAD ON PALMER ROAD BETWEEN SULLIVAN AND COUNTY LINE ROAD.
